Whether cleanup must fix this conceptual misrepresentation or only smooth the prose around it is the crux of the ambiguity.","R1.2":"The logical chain breaks openly: the abstract and discussion claim the system 'automatically compensates' for deviations in input statistics and 'guarantees monotone descent,' but the methods section describes no inference-time adaptation whatsoever. Any prose polish that leaves this contradiction intact risks laundering a false claim into more authoritative-sounding sentences.","R1.6":"'Clean this up' most naturally indexes writing polish — sentence flow, redundancy removal, word choice — making this the highest-probability primary responsibility regardless of whether substantive issues are also in scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'clean this up' address only surface-level prose (sentence flow, word choice, redundancy), or should it also resolve the substantive logical inconsistency between the Lyapunov adaptive-control framing and the static fixed-threshold implementation that is never updated at inference time?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact's core framing — Lyapunov stability, provable convergence, automatic compensation for distribution shift — is directly contradicted by an implementation that is a fixed binary classifier with a static threshold τ that is explicitly 'not updated at inference time.' Whether cleanup must fix this conceptual misrepresentation or only smooth the prose around it is the crux of the ambiguity.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain breaks openly: the abstract and discussion claim the system 'automatically compensates' for deviations in input statistics and 'guarantees monotone descent,' but the methods section describes no inference-time adaptation whatsoever. Any prose polish that leaves this contradiction intact risks laundering a false claim into more authoritative-sounding sentences.
rationale.R1.6: 'Clean this up' most naturally indexes writing polish — sentence flow, redundancy removal, word choice — making this the highest-probability primary responsibility regardless of whether substantive issues are also in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'clean this up' address only surface-level prose (sentence flow, word choice, redundancy), or should it also resolve the substantive logical inconsistency between the Lyapunov adaptive-control framing and the static fixed-threshold implementation that is never updated at inference time?
